feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Standard Return Order - Issues
entry.content:
We have the below issues for Standard Return Order

  1. If we want to create a Standard Return Order of 100+ line items, it becomes difficult to put 4-6 field mandatory data for each and every line item as shown in the attached file.
  2. The auto creation of Delivery and Invoice should be stopped.
  3. Credit Memo should not be posted automatically.
  4. FOC should be enable for Lean Return Order.

Please provide us with a solution for SAP S/4 Hana public cloud.

CB User - CB9980000000

entry.title: Unable to assign zero value to dummy SKU for shipping-only refund (S/4HANA Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi team,

We are integrating Shopify with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and using the Credit Memo Request (A2X API) to process refunds. Our requirement is to refund only shipping charges, without refunding the product. Based on earlier guidance, we created a dummy material (SKU) to represent the refund item.

However, we are facing an issue where we are not able to assign a value of 0.00 to the dummy SKU (base price condition PPR0). The system requires a positive value (e.g., 0.01).

Our questions are:

  1. Why is a dummy SKU required instead of using the original material from the invoice?
  2. Is there a supported way to allow zero-value pricing for this dummy item?
  3. If a separate pricing procedure is required for refunds, how can it be assigned in Public Cloud given that new document types cannot be created?

Any guidance or best practices for this scenario would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: The condition class in S4 HANA is not created BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 but it is in R3
entry.content:
Good morning everyone.
Currently, my client is in the process of migrating to S/4HANA. We have a program Z that creates sales orders (VA01) through the BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2. The program fills in all the required fields of the BAPI (it enters four condition classes per item), but after creating the order, it does so without the ZCGM condition, which is manually configured as mandatory for a specific order class, ZC06. If I create the order manually using VA01, SAP prompts me to enter the ZCGM condition class since it is mandatory, and it creates the other classes without problems.

The curious thing here is that this same BAPI is used in R/3, and the same fields are filled in, but the order is created using the ZCGM condition class.

I have seen that at the configuration level, everything is OK in both R/3 and S/4HANA. I performed standard debugging on the BAPI and verified that the condition is never lost, but for some reason, it's not created in S4 HANA when other condition classes are added. If only the ZCGM class is used, it creates it without problems, but the client needs to add other condition classes as well.

S4 HANA order created through BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2  (The ZCGM condition is not present.)

Order created in R3 with the same data and using BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2( if created with condition ZCGM )

 

Data that I enter in conditions for the BAPI in both S4 HANA and R3 environments.

We have also tried setting a value in LOGICAL_SWITCH-PRICING but it doesn't work for us in S4 HANA either.

entry.title: Split Valuation with Automatic Batch (Category ‘X’) – Valuation Type Issue in STO (ZUB)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have configured Split Valuation with activation at the valuation area level. For our materials, we are using valuation category ‘X’ (Automatic Batch), where each batch is automatically treated as a separate valuation type. This configuration is working as expected in standard scenarios.

However, we are facing an issue during the creation of a Stock Transport Order (STO) with the following details:

  • Document Type: ZUB
  • Material: 1400000005
  •

entry.title: Sales Organization address fields in in Email Templates
entry.content:
Hello,

Due to business requirements, there's the need to use sales organization address fields (Name 1, Name 2, etc...), however I cannot find these fields when extending the standard email template SD_SLS_DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATE and creating my own custom email template.

Are these fields available? If not, is there any other way to use it?

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: BC Upgrade TR import issue
entry.content:
BC Upgrade TR and BC scope extention TR (Owner CBC) on public cloud error while importing on QA/Test and PRD both.
Unable to re-import or manual import. Please advise.

entry.title: Supplier invoice clear issue-items have not been activated due to inconsistent withholding tax info
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

When we generated the invoice, the WHT details had not been entered in the 'Supplier Master.' However, we have now updated the WHT details in the Supplier Master. After entering the WHT information, we attempted to clear this invoice using a 'down payment' via the 'Clearing Outgoing Payments' application. At this stage, we encountered an issue; we received an error message stating, "Items were not activated due to inconsistent withholding tax information."

Based on my analysis, it appears that the absence of WHT details at the time the invoice was generated is the root cause of this problem.

How can we resolve this issue? Please provide appropriate advice.

Thanks & Regards,

DNR

entry.title: Provide a CDS view for Overhead rate in Costing Sheet
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

My project utilizes a costing sheet based on overhead quantity. At the end of each month, a step must be taken to input the actual overhead rate into the system, based on the overhead group maintained. This allows the customer to request a report to verify the actual overhead rate. Please provide a CDS view for the overhead rate in the costing sheet that you have released in the SAP public cloud.

Warm regards,

Chi

entry.title: Is there any api that can approve purchase orders in public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi All

We need to approve purchase oder through an extranal system,

Is there any api available in public cloud that supports purchase order

approval , or is there another way to handle approvals via en extranal system?

I found the nots 3551832 - Purchase Requisition approval via API and that mentions Purchase Requisition not have api for approval .However,it does not mention whether purchase oder are supported.

entry.title: Depreciation Start Date Requirement – Beginning of Fiscal Year (Irrespective of Capitalization Date)
entry.content:
We have a business requirement where the depreciation start date of an asset should always be set to the beginning of the fiscal year, irrespective of the capitalization month.

For example:

  • Capitalization Date: 15-Nov-2026
  • Expected Depreciation Start Date: 01-JAN-2026 (Start of Fiscal Year)

    We would like to confirm:

    1. Whether it is possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) to automatically set depreciation start date as start of fiscal year for all assets.
    2. If yes, please confirm the standard configuration steps.
    3. If not, is there any:

entry.title: Cost Center Derivation on Non-Cost Element GR/IR Account (SAP Cloud ERP)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system, a GR/IR account that is not defined as a cost element (i.e., no primary/secondary cost element category maintained) is unexpectedly deriving a cost center during journal entry posting. 

Analysis Performed:

  • Verified that the GR/IR account is not maintained as a cost element in controlling.
  • Checked configuration under account assignment settings and document splitting – no relevant configuration found that would enforce cost center derivation.
  • Reviewed substitutions/validations (if applicable) – no rule identified causing this behavior.
  • No custom logic (BAdI / extensibility) impacting account assignment.

We kindly request confirmation on whether this behavior is standard in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. In case this is not the intended standard behavior, please provide a suitable solution approach, relevant SAP Note, or correction instructions. Additionally, we would appreciate guidance on any recommended configuration settings or control mechanisms to prevent similar occurrences in the future.

BR,
Evrim

entry.title: Workflow Trigger Behavior After Project Approval
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a question regarding the standard workflow behavior for project approval.

If the workflow is triggered when a user changes the project status to “Released,” what happens after the approval is completed?
If the user modifies the project data again afterward, will the workflow be triggered again?

Additionally, if we plan to extend the approval process to an external BPM system, what would be the recommended approach to prevent the project creator from manually releasing the project?

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Customizing issue - Person Responsible
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA FIORI Cloud Public we have an issue regarding the order transfert between both systems : A8L(100 and 120) to A7F(120).

Customizing for :

  • « Person Responsible », ID 102966

is done in A8L/100, copied in A8L/120 but not copied in A7F/120 because we do not have any no transport order.

At saving, information message is displayed « Configuration changes are not recorded to a transport »

entry.title: 希望找到批量冲销的开发接口，及系统中的清账关系的批量冲销，希望得到您的帮助？
entry.content: 希望找到批量冲销的开发接口，及系统中的清账关系的批量冲销，希望得到您的帮助？I hope to find the development interface of batch write-off and batch write-off of the settlement relationship in the system, and I hope to get your help?

entry.title: The Requested By field in My Purchase Requisition app is not available in Manage PR Prof
entry.content: The Requested By field in My Purchase Requisition app is not available in Manage Purchase Requisition Professional app, how to make this field available in the app

entry.title: PRODUCTION/QC/SALES TRANSACTIONS DONE THROUGH BARCODE SCANNING
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We have the requirement to map in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition; I'm sharing the same need guidance how can we execute this in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud 

Requirement - 

 

PP / QM Process Understanding:

  • After completion of machining, the finished goods (FG) material should be carefully placed into the designated trolley. Ensure that the material is loaded strictly according to the trolley’s defined capacity to avoid damage, overloading, or handling issues.

 

  • Once the trolley is properly loaded, it should be transferred to the Quality Inspection area in a safe and controlled manner.

 

  • In the Quality area, the Quality Inspector performs a thorough inspection of the products to verify that they meet all required specifications and standards. Only the accepted (OK) quantity is considered for further processing. Based on this approved quantity, a QR code is generated.

 

  • Storage Location is Mandatory

 

  • The generated QR code must accurately capture and display all essential production details, including Production Order Number, Machine Number, Product Name, and Quantity. This ensures complete traceability and data integrity.

 

  • QR code generation is strictly dependent on stock availability in the system. The system should allow QR code generation only after the stock has been officially created and recorded. If the stock is not available in the system, QR code generation must be restricted to prevent discrepancies.

 

  • After successful QR code generation, the Quality Inspector securely affixes the QR code label onto the trolley. The labeled trolley is then moved to the Finished Goods (FG) area, where it is stored or prepared for dispatch.

 

 

 

SD Process Flow

Sales Order (VA01 / Fiori App)
↓
QR Scan App (Custom Fiori)
↓
Outbound Delivery Creation (API / BAPI)
↓
Picking (Auto)
↓
PGI (Post Goods Issue)
↓
Billing Document (Auto)
↓
E-Invoice + E-Way Bill (India Localization)
↓
Digital Signature
↓
Reference Number (Custom Object)

 

  1. Custom Fiori App + Custom Business Object (CBO) For QR Code Scanning
  2. Need to create custom app or Z table to store the below data

Fields:

  • QR Code
  • Material
  • Quantity
  • Plant
  • Storage Location
  • Sales Order
  • Vehicle Number
  • Status
  • Reference Number

QR Code contain - Material + Batch + Qty + Plant

 

The approach for delivery Business Object if available for Delivery creation and PGI for both (Need to check feasibility from Technical end)

 

OR

           Handling Unit (better option if EWM used)

           Create Custom Fiori App for QR scanning

 

Scan QR
Enter:

Customer Code – User will enter manually (Ref Customer Code system will automatically pick active SO)
Sales Order – System must automatically pick the current month’s Sales order based on the Material Code and Customer Code
Customer PO No – System must display automatically once sales order is picked

Vehicle Number – User will enter manually
Validate stock

Invoice No – Display

E-Invoice - Display

E-way Bill No - Display

ASN No – Display

System must generate a single Invoice against multiple QR codes of same Material and Sales Order

Check Box required for each Invoice Line so that user can only select the Invoices who’s ASN is generated. Remaining Invoices must be available for ASN generation and can be added in different Reference Number for this Add Button required

Cancelled Invoices must not appeared in the Pending List of Invoices appearing after clicking Add Button

Invoices for which E-Invoice, E-way bill successfully are allowed to print –Print Button required (Multiple)

Download Button required to download Invoice PDF (Multiple)


Store entries in Z custom table or can create custom CDS to store the data

To create delivery and PGI need to use Business Object or EWM handling unit (Best option)

  1. Use the Outbound Delivery (A2X) API to create an outbound delivery.
  2. Include the necessary information such as the sales order number and item quantity.
  3. The system will perform checks for restricted values and authorizations.
  4. Once the outbound delivery is created, the system will send the delivery number and data.
  5. To post goods issue, utilize the function within the API to reverse goods issue if needed.

To Create Billing Document same approach Business Object / API - BADI (SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER)

For E-invoice automation we have already configuration node so using configuration we can achieve this

For Eway bill we have BADI using same we can integrate E-way Bill directly to government portal and achieve this

 

 

For E-way bill automation need to use below details

Name : BADI_EDOCUMENT_IN_EWB
Method: IF_EDOCUMENT_IN_EWB~CHANGE_HEADER_DETAILS
Structure: EDOC_IN_EWB_TRANS_DETAILS

For Digital signature we have already used one BADI which is provided by SAP – need to check the feasibility of Automation as currently business doing the same process after invoice generation using app VF02

 

Custom CDS to store the Trip Details

Billing Document

Reference Number

After analysis I thought if we can set the background job to VL10A and Create Billing document to create Automatic Delivery PGI and Billing Document (If possible, if yes how to do this)

Looking forward to hearing from community team.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud J62: Asset Quantity Resulting from Direct Capitalization with Multiple Componen
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

while working with Scope Item J62 in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, we observed the following behavior during direct capitalization from procurement.

If an asset is created from multiple PR or PO items that logically form one physical asset, the resulting asset master shows the quantity as the sum of the component quantities. From a business perspective, however, there is only one asset. In this example it’s n standing desk, which has several components.

 

 

 

As far as we know, this is standard SAP behavior in the Public Cloud. However, this can be confusing for asset inventory, reporting, and overall asset transparency.

I would be interested to hear your experiences: Have you encountered the same scenario? Are there recommended best practices or workarounds?

We have also raised this topic via a Customer Influence request to suggest a more flexible solution. Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence


Diana

entry.title: Requirement to have Business Section Code Mandatory in Miro
entry.content:
Hi team 

My client need Business Section Code filed are PERSON_NAME in MIRO Screen ,kindly let you know how make PERSON_NAME filed in business second and any BAPI, Customization option are available in this process 

 

Thanks

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: IFRS 16 implementation via Scope Item 1T6 in a single-ledger setup — impacts and best practices
entry.content:
Dear community,

I'm working on an S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation for a logistics group (multiple companies: warehousing, transportation, port terminals, inspection services) and would like to validate our financial reporting architecture with the community.

Architecture overview:

  • Leading Ledger 0L — official IFRS reporting basis, covering both IFRS 15 (Revenue) and IFRS 16 (Leases)
  • Extension Ledger — management view only, excluding IFRS 16 effects so that lease contracts are reflected as direct rental expense (the way the board prefers to read the P&L). It does not serve any statutory standard.

Scope items under evaluation:

  • 1T6 — Lease-In Accounting (confirmed): to handle IFRS 16 in 0L
  • 3KK — Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR): under evaluation against standard J58 for IFRS 15
  • J58 — Accounting and Financial Close (baseline): already in scope

The IFRS 15 question — J58 or 3KK?

The customer's business is mostly event-based revenue recognition (services rendered = revenue recognized), which J58 would cover well. However, we've identified scenarios that may push us toward 3KK:

  • Take-or-pay contracts in port and warehousing operations (reserved capacity + variable usage), which seem to imply multiple performance obligations within a single contract
  • Deferred revenue scenarios where contract signature/invoicing date differs from the period in which the service is delivered
  • Potential bundling of services (storage + handling + inspection) sold as a package with a single price

I'd appreciate the community's input on the following points:

  1. IFRS 15 — when does 3KK become essential vs. J58? For those who implemented logistics or capacity-based businesses with take-or-pay clauses, did J58 standard hold up, or did you need to bring in 3KK / RAR? At what point did the multi-PO allocation, contract modifications, or breakage treatment force the move to RAR?
  2. 1T6 — Chart of Accounts impact: beyond the standard accounts that come with the reference CoA, what additional GL accounts have you typically needed to create for ROU Assets, Lease Liabilities (current/non-current split), depreciation of ROU, and interest expense on lease liabilities? Any account determination pitfalls worth flagging?
  3. Extension ledger for management view: for those who used an extension ledger to deliver a "pre-IFRS 16" management view, what was your approach — automated reversing entries, manual postings, or a reporting-only solution? How did you keep the extension ledger reconciled over time as new lease contracts are added or modified?
  4. Transition of legacy contracts (IFRS 16): full retrospective vs. modified retrospective — what worked best in your projects? Any lessons learned on initial measurement of ROU and liability?
  5. Apps and process: which Fiori apps from 1T6 (and from 3KK, for those who activated it) have you found most useful in day-to-day operation — contract creation, valuation runs, posting runs, reconciliation, reporting?
  6. Scope decisions: how have you handled IFRS 16 short-term lease and low-value asset exemptions in practice within the Public Cloud setup?

Any references to SAP Best Practices documentation, blog posts, or real-project experiences would be very welcome.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Assignment of Existing Sales Group to Another Sales Office in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

I would like to request guidance regarding the assignment of a Sales Group in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Currently, a Sales Group is already assigned to a specific Sales Office. We need to understand how to assign this same Sales Group to an additional Sales Office.

From our analysis in CBC, it appears that once a Sales Group is assigned to a Sales Office, no further changes or reassignments are possible. 

regards

Daniele
